purpose: Determine whether a caller owns or is permitted to access a specific resource so APIs can reject cross-user or cross-tenant access before reading or mutating protected data.

endpoints.description: Check whether a principal may access a resource based on ownership or delegated rights.

sidecar_specification.purpose: Guard resource access using ownership and delegated authorization rules.

agent_usage.when_to_use: Use before serving or mutating a resource that should only be visible to its owner or a privileged role.
agent_usage.when_not_to_use: Do not use as a full policy engine, identity provider, or role assignment service.
agent_usage.example_workflow: 1) Resolve the resource owner. 2) Call /security/ownership/check with principal and owner information. 3) Continue only when allowed is true.

common_calls.description: Check whether a user owns a generation

failure_modes.condition: Principal does not match the resource owner and lacks any overriding role
failure_modes.symptom: Access denied with an owner mismatch reason
failure_modes.recovery: Authenticate as the correct owner or use a role explicitly allowed by policy

forbidden_behaviors.description: Must not write outside declared scope
Must not make external network calls

agent_notes.implementation_hint: Accept resolved owner identifiers and optional role overrides, then return explicit allow or deny reasons such as owner_match, admin_override, or owner_mismatch.
agent_notes.known_gotchas: Ownership checks are often duplicated ad hoc in handlers; centralizing them avoids inconsistent cross-resource security behavior.
agent_notes.preferred_approach: Keep ownership validation pure and feed it already-resolved identity and resource metadata.
agent_notes.avoid_approach: Do not fetch arbitrary resources or perform authentication handshakes inside the guard.
